settings.title: Settings
settings.siteIdentityDesc: Customize your workspace name and browser tab icon
settings.siteIdentityAdminOnly: Only administrators can change the site identity
settings.developerDesc: Runtime debug switches for administrators. Enabling them increases log output; only turn them on while troubleshooting.
settings.debugFilesQueryHint: When enabled, each "file manager" list request prints the parsed query / WHERE clause / param count to the backend log — useful for diagnosing filters that don't seem to apply. Takes effect on all nodes within 30 seconds.
settings.debugFilesQueryEnvActive: Forced on via environment variable DEBUG_FILES_QUERY=1; this toggle cannot turn it off.
settings.panelLoadFailedHint: Try switching to another tab or reopening Settings. See console for details.
settings.iconTooLarge: Icon file must be less than 1MB
settings.switchesDesc: Manage feature switches and default behavior in one place
settings.themeModeDesc: Choose light, dark, or follow system
settings.editorFontDesc: Choose built-in font or import custom font
settings.prefNoteTitleAsAppTitle: Show current note title in tab
settings.prefNoteTitleAsAppTitleHint: When enabled, the browser tab / Electron window title shows "Note title - Site name", making it easier to tell windows apart. When disabled, only the site name is shown.
settings.prefOutlineDefaultOpenHint: When enabled, the outline panel on the right side is expanded every time you open a note. You can still toggle it manually at any time.
settings.prefLockOnOpenHint: When enabled, every note opens in read-only view; click the unlock button to start editing. This lock applies only on this device, is not written to the note, and does not affect other users.
settings.prefShowNotesInNotebookTreeHint: When enabled, the left notebook tree shows direct notes under expanded notebooks. Search, tags, favorites, and trash still use the list.
settings.readingDensityDesc: Adjust vertical spacing of paragraphs and list items. Compact saves screen space (per-device).
settings.importFontHint: Supports OTF / OTC / TTC / TTF / WOFF2 · Batch selection
settings.fontPreviewEn: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.

twoFactor.title: Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)
twoFactor.descriptionDisabled: Once enabled, signing in requires an additional 6-digit code from your Authenticator app
twoFactor.descriptionEnabled: Enabled. Sign-in requires a code from your Authenticator app.
twoFactor.setupHint: Scan the QR code with Google Authenticator, Microsoft Authenticator, 1Password, etc. — or enter the secret manually.
twoFactor.activateLabel: Enter the 6-digit code from the app
twoFactor.recoveryCodesTitle: Save these recovery codes (shown only once)
twoFactor.recoveryCodesHint: If you lose your Authenticator, each code can replace a code one time. Store them offline.
twoFactor.regenerateHint: Regenerating immediately invalidates all old codes
twoFactor.disableHint: Enter your current 6-digit code or a one-time recovery code to confirm

sessions.title: Active Sessions

userManagement.title: User Management
userManagement.description: Manage all user accounts in this workspace
userManagement.deleteConfirm: Delete user "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
userManagement.deleteModeCascade: Delete user and all their data
userManagement.deleteModeCascadeDesc: Irreversible. Notebooks, notes, workspaces, tasks, etc. will be permanently purged.
userManagement.deleteModeTransfer: Transfer data to another user, then delete
userManagement.deleteModeTransferDesc: Recommended for offboarding / account merge. Notebooks, notes, tags, tasks, moments, share links, and owned workspaces will be reassigned to the selected user.
userManagement.deleteTransferSuccess: Data of "{{name}}" was transferred; the account has been deleted.
userManagement.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 6 characters
userManagement.sudoDesc: This is a high-risk action. Please re-enter your login password to continue.
userManagement.sudoRateLimited: Too many requests, please try again later
userManagement.openRegistrationDesc: When disabled, new users cannot self-register on the login page
userManagement.fieldPersonalExportEnabledDesc: When disabled, this user cannot export notebooks or all notes from their personal space (workspace exports are unaffected; admin accounts always bypass this).
userManagement.fieldPersonalImportEnabledDesc: When disabled, this user cannot import notes into their personal space (workspace imports are unaffected; admin accounts always bypass this).

workspaceManagement.title: Workspace Management
workspaceManagement.description: Browse and manage all workspaces. Admins can edit or delete any workspace across users.
workspaceManagement.deleteConfirmHint: After deletion, notebooks and notes in this workspace will be moved back to the personal space of owner "{{owner}}". Members and invite codes will be removed. This action cannot be undone.
workspaceManagement.deleteConfirmHintNoOwner: After deletion, notebooks and notes in this workspace will be moved back to the owner's personal space. Members and invite codes will be removed. This action cannot be undone.

dataManager.title: Data Management
dataManager.description: Import and export your note data — your data belongs to you

scope.personalHint: Operations only affect your personal-space data (notes, notebooks).
scope.workspaceHint: Operations only affect the current workspace "{{name}}".
scope.workspaceNeedSwitch: Switch to a collaborative workspace from the sidebar first, then use this tab.
scope.systemHint: Database, Backup and Danger Zone are full-database operations (covering all users, all workspaces and personal spaces) — not scoped to any single workspace.
scope.adminOnly: Data Management is restricted to system administrators.
scope.personalExportDisabled: The administrator has disabled "Personal-space export". The export button is currently unavailable.
scope.personalImportDisabled: The administrator has disabled "Personal-space import". Import entries are currently unavailable.

dataManager.exportDescription: Export all notes as Markdown files organized by notebooks into a ZIP archive. Includes YAML frontmatter metadata, compatible with Obsidian, Typora, etc.
dataManager.exportInlineImagesHint: Off by default: images are written to an assets/ folder so the .md files stay small and preview correctly in Typora/Obsidian. When on, images are inlined as base64 — the export is larger, but re-importing the archive back into this app is much more reliable. Recommended for backup / migration.
dataManager.importDescription: Import Markdown (.md), plain text (.txt), HTML (.html), PDF (.pdf) files or ZIP archives. Compatible with Xiaomi Notes, OPPO/OnePlus Memo, vivo Notes export formats.
dataManager.dropFilesHere: Drop files here, or click to select
dataManager.pdfTooLarge: PDF "{{file}}" exceeds the {{limit}} MB limit. Please split it before importing.
dataManager.pdfNoTextLayer: No extractable text found in PDF "{{file}}" (likely a scanned/image PDF). Please run an OCR tool to convert it to a text-layer PDF before importing.
dataManager.perFileNotebookHint: Create a notebook named after each file and place the file inside it.
dataManager.duplicateMerge: Merge files with the same name into one notebook
dataManager.importDoneSwitchHint: The sidebar is not currently on this workspace — switch to see the imported notes.
dataManager.dangerDescription: This will permanently delete all notes, tasks, tags, and notebooks, and reset the admin account. Export a backup first. This action cannot be undone!
dataManager.resetConfirmTitle: Are you sure you want to erase all data?
dataManager.resetConfirmDesc: This will permanently erase all contents in the database, including:
dataManager.resetItem3: Admin password will be reset to admin123
dataManager.incorrectVerification: Please enter the correct verification word
dataManager.sudoPasswordHint: Factory reset requires password re-verification to prevent token abuse.

dataFile.title: Database File (.data)
dataFile.description: Full-database export/import at the SQLite file level. Best for machine migration or offline archival.
dataFile.exportDescription: Download the full SQLite database (.data) file. It contains every user's data — keep it safe.
dataFile.importDescription: Upload a .data file to replace the current database. Existing data will be overwritten after an automatic safety backup. Backend must restart after success.
dataFile.importSuccess: Import succeeded. Safety backup {{backup}} created. Please restart the backend now.
dataFile.importNotSqlite: The selected file is not a valid SQLite database (.data)
dataFile.importConfirmDesc: This replaces the entire database — every user's data will be overwritten. A safety backup of the current database will be created first.
dataFile.maintenanceDescription: Clean up orphan attachments (physical files) and compact the database to reclaim disk space. Recommended after emptying the trash.
dataFile.cleanupOrphansSuccess: Done: DB orphans {{dbRows}} (files {{dbFiles}}), content orphans {{contentRows}} (files {{contentFiles}}), disk orphans {{diskFiles}}; freed {{totalSize}}
dataFile.healthIssues: Attachment health issues found: {{missing}} missing physical files, {{dangling}} dangling content references
dataFile.healthMissingFilesTitle: DB records exist, but physical files are missing
dataFile.healthHint: The health check itself is read-only. Phase D repair actions require admin re-authentication: missing files can be repaired by uploading a replacement file; dangling references can be removed in batch to stop 404 requests. Shared files are normal after deduplication and are shown for reference-count diagnostics.
dataFile.repairSudoDesc: Attachment repair writes files or modifies note content. Enter the current admin password to continue.
dataFile.repairUploadConfirmDesc: Use {{file}} ({{size}}) to repair the missing physical file for attachment {{id}}: {{filename}}. If multiple records share the same physical file, they will be restored together.
dataFile.repairUploadSuccess: Attachment file restored: {{rows}} records affected, wrote {{size}}
dataFile.repairDanglingConfirmTitle: Remove dangling attachment references from content?
dataFile.repairDanglingConfirmDesc: This will remove {{refs}} URLs pointing to non-existing attachments from {{notes}} notes. Normal attachments will not be deleted.
dataFile.repairDanglingSuccess: Dangling references removed: {{refs}} refs across {{notes}} notes
dataFile.scanOrphansResult: Reclaimable {{reclaimable}} (DB orphans {{dbCount}} · content orphans {{contentCount}}); attachments dir total {{total}}
dataFile.scanOrphansEmpty: No orphan attachments found; attachments dir total {{total}}

backup.title: Backup & DR
backup.description: Snapshot (zip) of database + attachments + fonts + plugins. Point BACKUP_DIR to a separate physical volume; otherwise one disk failure loses both data and backups.
backup.importHint: Add a .bak / .zip backup received via email, USB stick, or from another instance to the list. Your live data is NOT touched — click "Restore" on the list entry later to actually apply it.
backup.degradedDesc: Backups have failed repeatedly or no success for a long time. Check BACKUP_DIR permissions and disk space.
backup.sameVolumeTitle: Backups on the same volume as data
backup.sameVolumeDesc: Cannot survive a volume-level failure. Configure BACKUP_DIR in docker-compose to point to a separate volume or remote path.
backup.notWritableDesc: BackupManager cannot write to {{dir}}; auto backups will keep failing.
backup.deleteConfirm: Delete this backup? This cannot be undone.
backup.sudoPrompt: Enter your current password to confirm this backup/restore action (only required once per 5 minutes)
backup.dirSameVolumeHint: The current backup directory is on the same physical volume as the data directory, so it cannot protect against volume-level failures. Switch to a separate volume or remote location.
backup.dirInputLabel: New backup directory (absolute path inside the container, e.g. /mnt/backup-volume)
backup.dirSwitchConfirm: Write all future backups to {{path}}?\n(Existing backup files will NOT be auto-migrated.)
backup.dirSwitchSuccess: Switched to {{path}}. Note: existing backup files were NOT auto-migrated.
backup.dirMigrateHint: Switching does not auto-migrate old backup files. Use docker exec cp manually if you want to keep them.
backup.intervalHint: Recommend ≥ 6 hours; too short steals IO from users, too long widens the recovery window. Click Save to apply; persists across restarts.
backup.dailyAtHint: Backup runs once daily at this time; pick an off-peak slot. Restarts won't trigger immediately — it waits for the next slot.
backup.keepCountHint: Only db-only auto backups are pruned; full backups are kept. Manual backups also trigger pruning. Range 1–100.
backup.autoEmailLabel: Email backup file after successful run
backup.autoEmailHint: Requires SMTP configured below. If attachment exceeds 25MB, sends a text-only notice. Silently skipped when SMTP is disabled.
backup.autoEmailInvalid: Please enter a valid recipient email
backup.restoreDangerDesc: Restore replaces the entire database (notes/tasks/diary/attachments of ALL users). The backend takes a pre-restore safety backup before overwriting, so a manual rollback is still possible.
backup.confirmFilenamePrompt: To prevent accidents, type the full backup filename:
backup.restoreDoneDesc: Database replaced. Restart the backend process to make sure all connections reopen the new file.
backup.sendEmailTooltip: Send this backup as an email attachment
backup.sendEmailNotePlaceholder: e.g. Monthly DR archive — keep ≥ 90 days
backup.sendEmailTooLarge: Backup exceeds the 25MB email attachment limit. Please use "Download" and send it manually.
backup.sendEmailGeneratedSuccess: Created new backup {{filename}} and sent to {{to}}: {{resp}}
backup.sendEmailFormatCurrentHint: Fastest — no new file is created; attachment size equals the current backup size.
backup.sendEmailFormatFull: Create & send a new .zip full backup
backup.sendEmailFormatFullHint: Packs database + attachments + fonts + plugins + JWT secret into .zip, sends it, and keeps it as a new backup entry. Largest size, fullest restore.
backup.sendEmailFormatDbOnly: Create & send a new .bak database snapshot
backup.sendEmailFormatDbOnlyHint: SQLite snapshot only (.bak) — no attachments/fonts/plugins. Smallest size, best suited for email.

smtp.title: Email channel (SMTP)
smtp.description: Configure an SMTP gateway to send backup files directly to any mailbox as an attachment — a fast, multi-device offline disaster-recovery path. The password is stored AES-GCM encrypted and never returned in plaintext.
smtp.secure: Use TLS (check for port 465; uncheck and rely on STARTTLS for 587)
smtp.passwordPlaceholder: Enter SMTP login password or app-specific password
smtp.passwordHint: Leave empty to keep the existing password unchanged.

guide.title: SMTP setup guide for common providers
guide.subtitle: QQ / 163 / Gmail / Outlook quick reference
guide.intro: Mainstream mailboxes no longer accept the regular login password for SMTP. You must first enable "client auth code / app-specific password" on the provider's site. The table below is a copy-ready reference; full click-by-click steps are in the linked docs.
guide.passAuthCodeQQ: 16-char auth code generated via SMS in QQ Mail settings
guide.passAuthCode163: Auth code generated after enabling IMAP/SMTP in mailbox settings
guide.passClientPass: Client-specific password generated in the enterprise mail console
guide.passAppPassword: App Password generated after enabling 2-step verification
guide.tipAuthCode: Put the auth code / App Password in the password field, NOT your mailbox login password — otherwise you'll hit 535 Authentication Failed.
guide.tipFromEqLogin: The "from email" must equal the login account, otherwise Office 365 / Gmail will reject the mail (550 / 553).
guide.tipPortTls: Always check "Use TLS" for port 465; uncheck it for 587 (the server will upgrade via STARTTLS automatically).
guide.tipAttachmentLimit: Per-email attachment cap is 25MB. If your backup exceeds it, switch to "create & send a new .bak snapshot" or download it and send manually.
guide.fullDocLink: Open the full guide (GitHub docs: step-by-step for QQ / 163 / Gmail / Outlook, Chinese)

tasks.addChildPlaceholder: Subtask title, press Enter to confirm...
tasks.confirmDeleteWithChildren: This task has {{count}} subtask(s). This cannot be undone. Delete?

urlImport.unsupportedUrl: Only WeChat public-account article links are supported for now (mp.weixin.qq.com/s/...)

miCloud.title: Mi Cloud Import
miCloud.description: Import notes directly from Mi Cloud using your Cookie. No manual export needed. Supports batch selection, one-click import, and automatic image downloading.
miCloud.cookieRequired: Please enter your Mi Cloud Cookie
miCloud.cookieInvalid: Cookie is invalid or expired, please get a new one
miCloud.helpStep1: Open i.mi.com in your browser and sign in with your Xiaomi account
miCloud.helpStep2: Navigate to the Notes page after login
miCloud.helpStep3: Press F12 to open Developer Tools, switch to the Network tab
miCloud.helpStep4: Refresh the page, click any request, find and copy the full Cookie from request headers
miCloud.cookieWarning: ?? Cookie is equivalent to your account credentials, do not share with others. Cookie is only stored in the current session and will be cleared when the page is closed.

oppoCloud.title: OPPO Cloud Notes Import
oppoCloud.description: Import notes from OPPO (ColorOS) cloud sticky notes. Extract data from the OPPO cloud website via browser console, then paste here to import.
oppoCloud.helpStep1: Open cloud.heytap.com in your browser and sign in with your OPPO account
oppoCloud.helpStep2: Navigate to the Sticky Notes page
oppoCloud.helpStep3: Press F12 to open Developer Tools, switch to the Console tab, paste the following script and press Enter:
oppoCloud.helpStep4: The script will try to extract notes automatically. If prompted to trigger data loading, click on the note categories on the left, then run window.__extractNotes() in the console
oppoCloud.helpStep5: After notes are copied to clipboard, come back to this page and paste into the input box below
oppoCloud.jsonPlaceholder: Paste the JSON data copied from the console here...
oppoCloud.jsonRequired: Please paste the notes JSON data
oppoCloud.invalidData: Invalid data format, please make sure you pasted the correct JSON data
oppoCloud.parseError: JSON parsing failed, please check the data format

iCloud.title: iPhone Notes Import
iCloud.description: Import notes from iPhone (iCloud) Notes. Supports extracting data via iCloud web or pasting JSON data directly.
iCloud.method1Step1: Open the Notes app on your Mac, select the notes you want to export
iCloud.method1Step2: Click menu "File → Export as PDF" or select text and copy/paste to .txt / .md files
iCloud.method1Step3: Use the "Import Notes" section above to import .txt / .md / .html files directly
iCloud.method2Title: Method 2: Extract via iCloud web console script
iCloud.method2Step1: Open icloud.com/notes in your browser and sign in with your Apple ID
iCloud.method2Step2: Press F12 to open Developer Tools, switch to the Console tab
iCloud.method2Step3: Paste the following script and press Enter to run:
iCloud.method2Step4: Wait for the script to extract notes one by one (do not interact with the page)
iCloud.method2Step5: After notes are copied to clipboard, come back to this page and paste into the input box below
iCloud.tip: ?? Method 1 is recommended for reliability. Method 2 depends on iCloud's DOM structure and may break if Apple updates the page.
iCloud.jsonPlaceholder: Paste the JSON data copied from the console here...
iCloud.jsonRequired: Please paste the notes JSON data
iCloud.invalidData: Invalid data format, please make sure you pasted the correct JSON data
iCloud.parseError: JSON parsing failed, please check the data format

youdao.title: Youdao Cloud Notes Import
youdao.description: Import notes from a local folder produced by Youdao Cloud Notes "Batch Export". Recognizes Markdown / Word / text / HTML notes plus PDF / images / archives as attachments, and rebuilds nested notebooks from folder structure.
youdao.howToExport: How to export from Youdao Cloud Notes?
youdao.helpStep1: Open Youdao Cloud Notes web (note.youdao.com) or desktop client
youdao.helpStep2: Right-click root or target folder → choose "Batch Export"
youdao.helpStep3: Wait for the export package to be downloaded as a local folder
youdao.helpStep4: Click the button below and pick that folder; the tool will scan it
youdao.helpTip: Tip: Youdao usually leaves an "export-failure-solution_*.txt" file in the folder. This tool converts it into a note so you don't lose the list.
youdao.rootNotebookHint: Imported notes will live under this root notebook, with nested notebooks rebuilt from folders
youdao.noFilesSelected: Please select at least one file

ai.customSavedEmpty: No saved instructions yet. Fill in the content and click Save.

server.title: Connect to Server
server.subtitle: Enter your Nowen Note server address to get started
server.addressHint: Pick a protocol and enter host and port (port is optional)
server.note: Make sure the server is running and reachable from this device

download.title: Downloads
download.subtitle: Get Nowen Note on desktop, mobile and NAS. Mainland China users can switch to a mirror for faster downloads.
download.proxyTip: Overseas users: connect to GitHub directly. Mainland China users: try Gitee mirror first (only small files like .exe / .apk / .zip / .deb, auto-synced by CI), then ghproxy public mirrors which also cover large files like .fpk / .upk. Public mirror availability varies — rotate as needed.
download.giteeFallbackTip: Not on Gitee — fell back to GitHub
download.unavailable: Failed to fetch the latest release
download.noAssets: No recognised installer in this release. See GitHub Releases for raw assets.
download.dockerDesc: Run a minimal self-hosted instance on your own server or NAS. After it starts, open http://<host>:3001 to finish setup.
download.dockerCnTip: Tip: if Docker Hub is slow in your region, replace the image prefix with a regional mirror.

about.title: About
about.description: An open-source, self-hosted note-taking app focused on privacy and productivity. Supports Markdown / rich-text editing, AI assistant, web clipping, mind maps, real-time sync, and team collaboration.
about.featureClipper: Browser clipper extension for one-click saving
about.featureSelfHost: One-click Docker deploy, full data ownership
about.sponsorDesc: If this project helps you, buy the author a coffee ?
about.sponsorTip: Scan with WeChat · buy the author a keyboard / a bug fix
about.changelogDesc: See this release's new features and past fixes
about.authorStoryDesc: The story behind the project · A NAS hobbyist's self-rescue
about.authorStoryLoadFailed: Failed to load the author's note

versions.title: Version History
versions.emptyHint: Versions are recorded automatically on save

tokens.title: Personal Access Tokens
tokens.subtitle: Create dedicated tokens for third-party apps (bookmark sync, CLI tools). Each can be revoked independently — no need to change your password.
tokens.banner: Tokens are equivalent to passwords. The plaintext is shown only once at creation — save it immediately. If you suspect a leak, revoke and create a new one.
tokens.revokeConfirmDesc: Any application using this token (including “{{name}}”) will lose access immediately. This action cannot be undone.

empty.title: No access tokens yet
empty.desc: Create a token to let third-party apps (such as the bookmark home sync or CLI tools) access your notes securely — without revealing your password.

create.title: Create access token
create.subtitle: The plaintext token will only be shown once. Please copy it before closing the window.
create.nameHint: For your own future reference. Not exposed to third parties.
create.neverExpiresHint: Tokens that never expire need extra care. Setting an expiration is strongly recommended.
create.errScopes: Please select at least one scope

created.title: Token created
created.warning: This is the only time you can see the full token.
created.warningDesc: Once you close this window, the plaintext can never be retrieved again. Copy and save it now.
created.tokenHint: Click the input to select all, or use the Copy button.
created.copyFail: Copy failed, please select and copy manually
created.closeConfirmDesc: The plaintext of this token will be permanently unavailable. Please confirm you have saved it.
